element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Boomer (clown character) introduces foundational combat with air jumps and tricks
Each fighter has unique move sets; players learn variations through progression
Guggler ostrich introduces boss-level aggression and beak-based attacks
Dinosaur's ground shock wave requires spatial awareness and timing adaptation
Genie transformations into multiple animal forms demand pattern recognition
Ultimate challenge combines self-reconstruction with high-damage physical attacks; two-phase encounter
Colored belts (Red, Green, Blue, Black) change character appearance; signals achievement ladder
Defeating final boss grants ability to play as previously-fought bosses (Guggler, Bounder, T-Wrecks, Lamprey)

typical_relief_triggers: Belt acquisition (visual reward after boss defeat)
typical_tension_triggers: Lamprey's swift magical attacks and animal transformations
Jester's two-phase encounter with high damage output
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Tournament ladder structure with escalating boss difficulty; 21 total matches across three difficulty levels create graduated tension buildup toward final Jester encounter

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory opponent variety (Boomer, Bruiser, Crusher) teaches basic mechanics with humorous character designs; Red Belt victory accessible
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Boss progression escalates from Guggler (pecking/tossing) through Bounder (boxing/tail balance) to T-Wrecks (ground shocks); Green and Blue Belt acquisition
difficulty_curve.late_game: Lamprey introduces magical complexity with animal transformations; Jester final boss demands mastery of all learned patterns plus handling two-phase assault with disassembly mechanic
notable_spikes: T-Wrecks introduces devastating ground shock wave attacks requiring spatial repositioning
Lamprey's rapid transformation ability prevents pattern memorization
Jester's two-phase encounter and self-reconstruction mechanic represents significant skill threshold

notable_level_patterns.description: Five sequential boss encounters (Guggler → Bounder → T-Wrecks → Lamprey → Jester) introduce increasingly complex attack patterns and special abilities; each defeat grants colored belt and cosmetic character change
notable_level_patterns.example_level: T-Wrecks encounter (third boss) - introduces ground-based AoE mechanics via tail shock waves

notable_level_patterns.description: Playable roster features humor-based mechanics (Boomer's air tricks, Yoko's flatulence, Divine's spanking) balanced against brute-force combatants (Bruiser, Crusher); boss fighters scale to multiple animal forms or transformative states
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Lamprey boss fight - genie shapeshifting into El Ballz (bull), Spike (scorpion), Byte Viper (snake)

notable_level_patterns.description: Three difficulty tiers across 21 matches culminate in Jester defeat, which grants playability of all five bosses as new characters; incentivizes replay and mastery
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Post-game boss character roster unlocked after final victory
